chief_complaint: A 59-year-old woman presented with vertigo. She had a history of acute coronary syndrome (ACS) 2 years previously.
history: The patient had a history of ACS without obstructive coronary lesions. She presented with vertigo and was found to have a left cerebellar embolism. Review of previous echocardiograms revealed a mobile aortic valve tumor, suggesting papillary fibroelastoma (PFE), which was present at the time of the ACS event.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are mentioned in the case report.
labs: MRI revealed a left cerebellar embolism. TEE detected a mobile aortic valve tumor. At surgery, the PFE was found to be obstructing the right coronary artery (RCA) ostium.
correct_diagnosis: Papillary Fibroelastoma of the Aortic Valve
differentials: Papillary Fibroelastoma of the Aortic Valve
correct_ranking: Papillary Fibroelastoma of the Aortic Valve
